name: Atomic Operations with Lua Scripts
description: Execute complex multi-step operations atomically in Redis using server-side Lua scripting to eliminate race conditions.
trendingReason: Lua scripting enables atomic transactions that replace MULTI/EXEC for complex logic in high-concurrency systems

steps.title: Run an inline Lua script
steps.description: Execute a simple Lua script that sets and returns a value atomically.
steps.command: redis-cli EVAL "redis.call('SET', KEYS[1], ARGV[1]) return redis.call('GET', KEYS[1])" 1 mykey hello
steps.tip: KEYS[1] is the first key argument, ARGV[1] is the first non-key argument. Always pass keys via KEYS for cluster compatibility.

steps.title: Atomic compare-and-swap
steps.description: Implement a compare-and-swap operation that only updates a key if it matches an expected value.
steps.command: redis-cli EVAL "if redis.call('GET', KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then redis.call('SET', KEYS[1], ARGV[2]) return 1 else return 0 end" 1 mykey hello world

steps.title: Load a script for reuse
steps.description: Load a Lua script into Redis and get its SHA hash for repeated execution without resending the script body.
steps.command: redis-cli SCRIPT LOAD "redis.call('SET', KEYS[1], ARGV[1]) redis.call('EXPIRE', KEYS[1], ARGV[2]) return 1"
steps.tip: Save the returned SHA hash and use EVALSHA to call the script by hash, reducing bandwidth.

steps.title: Execute a loaded script by SHA
steps.description: Run a previously loaded script using its SHA hash.

steps.title: Atomic rate limiter in Lua
steps.description: Implement a precise rate limiter as a single atomic Lua script.
steps.command: redis-cli EVAL "local count = redis.call('INCR', KEYS[1]) if count == 1 then redis.call('EXPIRE', KEYS[1], ARGV[2]) end if count > tonumber(ARGV[1]) then return 0 else return 1 end" 1 ratelimit:user1001 10 60

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Redis Lua Scripting Examples
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Inline script: SET + GET ==="
redis-cli EVAL "redis.call('SET', KEYS[1], ARGV[1]) return redis.call('GET', KEYS[1])" 1 lua:test "hello-lua"

echo ""
echo "=== Compare-and-swap ==="
redis-cli SET cas:key "old-value"
RESULT=$(redis-cli EVAL "if redis.call('GET', KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then redis.call('SET', KEYS[1], ARGV[2]) return 1 else return 0 end" 1 cas:key "old-value" "new-value")
echo "CAS result (1=swapped): $RESULT"
echo "New value: $(redis-cli GET cas:key)"

echo ""
echo "=== Load script and use EVALSHA ==="
SHA=$(redis-cli SCRIPT LOAD "redis.call('SET', KEYS[1], ARGV[1]) redis.call('EXPIRE', KEYS[1], tonumber(ARGV[2])) return 1")
echo "Script SHA: $SHA"
redis-cli EVALSHA "$SHA" 1 lua:cached "cached-data" 300
echo "Cached value: $(redis-cli GET lua:cached)"
echo "TTL: $(redis-cli TTL lua:cached)"

echo ""
echo "=== Atomic rate limiter ==="
RATE_SCRIPT="local count = redis.call('INCR', KEYS[1]) if count == 1 then redis.call('EXPIRE', KEYS[1], tonumber(ARGV[2])) end if count > tonumber(ARGV[1]) then return 0 else return 1 end"
for i in {1..12}; do
  ALLOWED=$(redis-cli EVAL "$RATE_SCRIPT" 1 lua:ratelimit 10 60)
  echo "Request $i: $([ "$ALLOWED" = "1" ] && echo "ALLOWED" || echo "DENIED")"
done

echo ""
echo "=== Cleanup ==="
redis-cli DEL lua:test cas:key lua:cached lua:ratelimit

faq.question: Are Lua scripts atomic in Redis?
faq.answer: Yes, Redis executes Lua scripts atomically. No other command runs while a script is executing. This makes Lua scripts a powerful alternative to MULTI/EXEC transactions for complex logic.

faq.question: What is the difference between EVAL and EVALSHA?
faq.answer: EVAL sends the full script body each time. EVALSHA sends only the SHA1 hash of a previously loaded script, saving bandwidth. Use SCRIPT LOAD first, then EVALSHA for frequently called scripts.

faq.question: Can Lua scripts call external services?
faq.answer: No, Redis Lua is sandboxed. Scripts can only call redis.call() and redis.pcall() to interact with Redis, plus use built-in Lua libraries (string, math, table, cjson). No network or file I/O is available.
